name: Halloween Chili
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 Pounds ground goblin gizzards (ground beef 15% fat)
1 15 Oz. Can soft shelled beetles (kidney beans)
1 28 Oz. Can blood of bat (V-8 juice)
1 Dash Redtailed hawk toenails (crushed red pepper)
Water from a stagnant pond (tap water)
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Substitutions are in parenthesis. Best made during the last phase of the moon, if that is not possible, just do the best you can in a softly lighted kitchen after dark.
1. Brown the gizzards in an iron cauldron over a fire made from the siding off of a haunted house.
2. Add chopped eye of Cyclops and simmer until the pieces become translucent.
3. Add blood of bat, soft shelled beetles, bring to a slow bubbling boil.
4. At this time, add common weed, dried maggots, toenails, sumac, grubs, hemlock, eye of newt, and pureed wasp.
5. Adjust the consistency with pond water as needed.
6. The chili is done when the eye of newt swells and a vertical tan-colored "cats eye" appears on one side.
